name: ii-Python/iiPythonx
description: The repository I use for editing my GitHub profile, although it also keeps all of my Linux dotfiles. Still active as of 2025.

name: Website (2020)
description: The original implementation of my website, hosted at iipython.cf.

name: Prism
description: The only discord bot you will ever need (satire), died after nearly 3 years of hard work on it. I quit making discord bots afterwards due to Discord's addition of the intent system.

name: RoBot
description: Keypress-based scriptable automation system for the Roblox client. All it was able to do was move around a little and was extremely janky, but yet it's my most starred repo.

name: Sprint
description: A custom shell meant to be minimal with commands, color support, multi-line editing, and more.

name: Throwaway Keys
description: A project that I designed to create secret keys for use in projects without needing to manually generate one.

name: Advent of Code
description: Project to handle my source code solutions for the 2021 Advent of Code.

name: Autofix
description: Background script to execute AUTORUN.INF files on flash drives, similar to old versions of Windows.

name: Cubic
description: A discord bot with an extensive economy system, designed partially as a test of new features before being implemented into Prism.

name: Custom Presence
description: Program that allowed users to set a custom discord status using pypresence.

name: DOS-Style Website
description: A website that was very reminiscent of the DOS era of computing. Page layout and system was incredibly inspired by qtqt's website from 2021 which you can see here.

name: Fetch
description: CLI tool to download files, similar to wget and curl, but written in pure Python with no dependencies. Never got killed, but I stopped working on it after dmed me and I ended up doing something else.

name: File Archive
description: A website that allowed me to archive files in a root folder and then authorize specific accounts to access it. The theme was based on NES.css, and ran for about 5 months.

name: Honknet
description: Honknet was a collaboration between me and DmmD where we attempted to make a chat system. It was later converted to Plasma, which died a year later.

name: JAUS
description: Just Another Url Shortener, a flask website that literally just acted as an old ass url shortener. Killed because it was slow and useless.

name: Minicraft RP
description: Program that provided discord rich presence for people playing Minicraft+.

name: Modbot
description: A discord bot that was centered around moderation, mostly chat filtering and was used for a very short time in a private discord server.

name: MyShell
description: A customizable shell program that allowed you to load custom 'kernels', giving full control over what was ran.

name: Neutron
description: A web logging system, complete with web interface and Python module. It would hook into your code and send all tracebacks to the server of your choosing for remote viewing.

name: OPP
description: A package manager that was intended to be cross platform and similar to apt.

name: PyHTTPFS
description: Flask based system that was meant to act as a static file sharing tool. Killed and removed because people kept using it and it was insecure.

name: PyNet (v1)
description: A raw socket service that allowed sharing 'webpages' using a TUI client along with a web interface for controlling and administering the pages.

name: PyNet (v2)
description: Basically a perfect copy of PyNet v1, but with a version bump and client changes.

name: PyNet (v3)
description: A full rewrite of PyNet, including a client, server, custom byte protocol, tooling, and web services to manage it all. This variant was never completed, but the remnants of it still linger in my head from time to time.

name: Sprint v2
description: Attempted rewrite of Sprint, but I never found the time to fix it. It was also plagued by unicode escape color bugs.

name: Tempmon
description: Script I made to monitor my CPU temps, because my PC was overheating while trying to play Half-Life 2.

name: Time Lock
description: Terminal app that utilized tapping keys in a timed pattern in order to unlock or access data.

name: Webchat
description: Web chat system I made while in school suspension so I could talk to my friends easier then trying to get on discord. The database was implemented with Redis, which was a mistake but it was my first time using it.

name: Windows Cleaner
description: Script that used powershell and registry editing to clean up and debloat a stock Windows 10 system.

name: iiPython.py
description: Python library where I shoved a bunch of 'useful' primitives, including a socket library, terminal utilities/coloring, and a keypress system.

name: iiPython Auth
description: A custom implementation of an OAuth2 providing system. Killed before being implemented into any other projects.

name: Bongo Shell
description: Attempt at a bash-compatible shell written in Nim, failed due to lack of motivation.

name: Dioxide
description: Tool that locks students out of ActiveStudent accounts. Had a week of weaponization against my local school district, before being shut down to prevent expulsion.

name: Disk Speedtest
description: Very small CLI tool that benchmarks connected flash drives using hdparm.

name: Down Site
description: A 'system offline' website that was meant to compliment my 2022 flat site design.

name: eDOS
description: Emulated operating system that was intended to be stored completely in a tarball.

name: English Sucks
description: Project that would run the Minecraft Java 1.18 language file through Google Translate around 7 times, then saved the result to a resource pack to make funny item names.

name: Enigma
description: TUI based Engima machine that was implemented using pyenigma as a school project.

name: Fedora95
description: Collection of bash scripts that turned Fedora Rawhide into a Chicago95 primed environment.

name: Hamsters
description: A project that was intended to essentially be an in-terminal game engine powered by pixel graphics. You were supposed to be able to scan a QR code visible on your screen and load a game off of that 'cartridge'. Killed because I couldn't find a good way to read from the screen.

name: Haze Cipher
description: A cipher that was intended to be reversible and hard to crack. It ended up being neither of those things, but at least I tried; it was removed from GitHub to prevent people from using it for serious data.

name: iiPythonator
description: Discord self-bot that was meant to play music and music only, and it did a damn good job of that.

name: iiPython Intranet
description: A web interface that would spin up reverse proxies at runtime for projects and handle authentication. I killed it off due to lack of time and motivation.

name: Website (2022-23)
description: The source code for my 2022-2023 website, which allowed users to define 'templates' and could switch their website template at any time. Some of my examples include my bear website and my flat design.

name: Image Loader v2
description: A Python web server that allowed Roblox servers to request images as comma seperated RGB values, so the image could be recreated using parts in-game.

name: Launch+
description: A single page app designed with Nitrogen (also dead) to act as Kiosk and provide apps such as weather access.

name: Nitrogen
description: Python module that was similar to Electron, allowing you to open web pages in Qt and send data to the Python backend.

name: p2p
description: Project that allowed sending files over an HTTP tunnel between two devices using Peer to Peer networking.

name: PHAT
description: A hardware based key system that was meant to use USB flash drives as authentication devices.

name: Rickroll
description: A flask site that literally just rickrolls you, nothing else.

name: Roblox Status Live
description: Collection of web services for monitoring the status of Roblox's vast network of servers and APIs. Included a collection agent, storage system, and frontend.

name: Service Launcher
description: CLI tool that acted very similar to systemd units. It took a config file defining what to run, and provided a daemon/CLI to interact with the processes.

name: Start Page
description: A startpage I made that was designed to be quick and minimal, originally designed for a member of one of my group chats.

name: StreamX
description: A project I helped design that acted as a self-hosted alternative to Roblox's StreamingEnabled. The original devforum thread is still available here.

name: Virtual Assistant
description: Python program that acted as a personal assistant running locally on your PC. No AI was used in it, I was using wildcard pattern matching which was just as bad as you can imagine.

name: WebTransfer
description: File transferring web application built with Flask, you just had to make an account and add a friend to send any sized file to them.

name: x2 Website
description: The official website for x2, which allowed users to publish packages and download others. At this point its useless, and x2 has turned into xpp.

name: xpp
description: Complete rewrite and modernization of both the X Programming Language, and what was formerly known as x2. Custom programming language inspired by Batch, x86 Assembly, and others.

name: 2023 Countdown
description: A project designed to perform a countdown until New Years 2023. Reused in 2024, with varying degrees of success due to date processing issues.

name: Album Downloader
description: Selenium-based utility to download entire albums from the now defunct free-mp3-download.net website.

name: Alpha
description: Mockup of a very extendable and easy to understand programming language, eventually turned into English.

name: Chungus
description: A pure-python math engine designed to be implemented into xpp.

name: CubeRPC
description: An extremely basic Discord RPC for musikcube.

name: GArchive
description: Code for the original website for Geesecraft. Replaced by DmmD's new Svelte-based code.

name: Goose NFT
description: Jokily made terminal NFT system where a user could sign text files using a Public/Private keypair and then verify the files.

name: ISM
description: iiPython System Manager, a web UI that was similar to Grafana, allowing you to keep a quick tab on your system.

name: Modrinth Modpack Updater
description: A CLI tool that would take a JSON file containing modpack information, and automatically update all the mods to a specific Minecraft version.

name: Ooga Booga
description: URL shortener that was meant to replace JAUS, rewritten using modern async libraries and all.

name: Shuttle
description: Chat system that was (essentially) meant to be Honknet v2, a collab between me and DmmD to make a decentralized, modern chat system. It is on an indefinite hold as of May 2025.

name: UU-Extended
description: Minecraft Java mod based on Fabric to extend UU-Matter from Tech Reborn.

name: Xenon
description: A Roblox streaming engine that was meant as a no-server-required rewrite of StreamX. It could stream anything except for attachment connections, and was much easier to implement. The original devforum thread is available here.

name: Codelet
description: A code giveaway platform for Roblox, intended to supersede 'FTS' giveaways. The project was made in conjunction with pyxfluff but never published.

name: English
description: Simple to use, english language based programming language. First project to be included in the Goos.lol organization.

name: Goos.lol
description: The official Goos.lol website, which is still visible on the wayback machine.

name: iiPython Core
description: Core service for all of my data and sites, meant to be a home for datasets. I abandoned it after getting the main UI up and indexing my music.

name: Musescore Downloader
description: A site that allowed users to download sheet music from Musescore via the use of dl-librescore.

name: Prism v3
description: The final variation of Prism, featuring slash commands, intents, and all the other garbage that Discord implemented. Killed because I grew sick and tired of making Discord bots.

name: Textnet
description: Another attempted rewrite of PyNet, this one included a terminal rendering solution that was somewhat modernized but otherwise didn't get very far.

name: Weather Archiver
description: An old website designed to constantly record data from OpenWeatherMap, store it, and then graph it with a neat UI.

name: igame
description: A python module for making terminal games, giving access to easy audio control, menu systems, and function timeouts. Implemented in some of K4ffu's projects, otherwise not used.
Assembly-inspired programming language that required manually managing the memory stack, dropping memory, etc.

name: Acellus
description: Collection of decompiled scripts of Acellus's web interface. Allowed speeding up videos, preventing tab detection, pasting into writing tutor, and more.

name: Album Art Downloader
description: Python script that will automatically scan my music directory for any albums without cover art, and will then open MusicHoarder's cover website.

name: Anime CLI
description: TUI script that streams anime directly from torrents, originally based on Consumet. It is one of the only projects that was published by Goos.lol.

name: Assistant
description: A remake of my original Virtual Assistant, this time using ChatGPT and actually implementing features such as weather, music control, etc.

name: cdbar
description: Website that displays information about various CDs given a barcode, powered by MusicBrainz.

name: Cephei
description: Intended to be a chat system, it started as a rewrite of the currently on hold Shuttle project. I implemented a login and signup flow and then got tired of it.

name: CRS
description: Central Routing System, a websocket based FastAPI project that was intended to be used to replace REST for some of my microservices and join them together.

name: Distributed Filesystem
description: Test implementation of a distributed filesystem across several different nodes spanning different IP networks. Never completed.

name: dnsbench
description: Quick script that benchmarks local DNS servers using dnspython.

name: Emojizer
description: Discord bot that communicates with gpt4free and responds to users with emojis only.

name: Feishin
description: My personal fork of jeffvli's Feishin, an Electron-based music player. This fork includes theming changes, QOL improvements, completely revamped Discord RPC, and much more.

name: Feishin Micro
description: Miniplayer for Feishin, that floats on your desktop and allows you to control the app without opening the entire window. Includes scrolling text UI, a song visualizer, and that's about it.

name: Feishin RPC
description: External RPC application for Feishin that was meant to improve the Discord RPC experience. This was later built into my custom fork so it is no longer maintained.

name: Fiku
description: Attempted rewrite of Maloja, with the goal of being MUCH more efficient. Goal completed, although I only got like 3 pages implemented.

name: File System
description: Attempt at a blazing fast, privacy centered, and modern file sharing service.

name: Halo
description: An attempted remake of Nova with the main selling point being speed.

name: RFS
description: A remote filesystem for copying files from system to system without SSH or something that requires a lot of setup.

name: LRCup
description: Python CLI and library for interacting with the LRCLIB.net API. It also includes tools for embedding lyrics, along with general LRC tools.

name: Navidrome Play Mover
description: Script that can move track plays between albums in Navidrome.

name: ndcli
description: Navidrome browsing CLI tool, implemented in Python and then later attempted in Go. Allowed checking track/album/artist statistics from the terminal.

name: NETGEAR UI
description: Replacement interface for the NETGEAR C7800 series router, since the default one sucks.

name: Website (Modern)
description: My latest modern website, which has still gone through nearly 4 different variations at this point.

name: Nightwatch
description: A chat system similar to all the ones I've made previously, but this time with a full server, complete client, bot library, web frontend, and still actively maintained.

name: Nova
description: A site building framework built using the Python websockets module, Jinja2 and no other dependencies. It's capable of auto minification, TypeScript compilation, and more.

name: Now Playing
description: A microservice for sending now playing information from ListenBrainz in a websocket. Actively in production use on my website.

name: OBS Feishin
description: A widget to display music information from Feishin in OBS Studio. Made in a night, and never used again.

name: Pizza
description: Metadata collection system and management tool, meant to compete primarily with beets.

name: Pizza Proxy
description: Cover art proxying system for use in my fork of Feishin, which allows users to get cover art in their RPC without opening ports.

name: Statuspage
description: Simple, fast as hell, efficient on resources multi-server status page powered by Cloudflare Pages. Killed due to me no longer needing a statuspage.

name: TP Link Lightbulb
description: Basic TP-Link lightbulb controller written with Electron, since I couldn't find any better solutions.

name: USPS
description: A fully featured USPS package tracker with additional support for UPS packages. I've used it to receive nearly 15 different packages over the past year.

name: DmmD OS
description: An in-browser operating system based on Windows 95, designed to be ran from within DmmD's BIOS.

name: dmmd-py
description: A python library/module to wrap all of 's APIs. As of right now that just includes his iCDN.

name: Networking
description: A Cloudflare-backed site that showed HTTP(s) connection statistics and routing information.

name: PNV3
description: The final form of PyNet, an actively in development rewrite of PyNet v3, hence keeping the original acronym.

name: Radar
description: Leaflet-powered web interface for the National Weather Service's radar API, because I couldn't find a site with a clean looking dark UI.

name: Radio
description: A next generation web radio project, powered by websockets and FastAPI.

name: Sound
description: Send files with sound or die trying: encode binary into hexadecimal, send as sound waves, receive on the other end and reconstruct the file.

name: htx
description: Zero-dependency HTTP server and reverse proxy built entirely from scratch as an experiment/proof of concept. It can act as a standalone HTTP server, giving low level access to the protocol, and also has reverse proxy functionality.

name: il
description: Python module for making clean logs, boxes, lines, and everything of the sort.

name: logic
description: A logic symbol processor for PHI-1113 that I wrote in the span of a couple weeks. My professor thought it was 'pretty neat'.

name: NJROTC Autograde
description: The goal was to create an application that allows for quick and easy autograding of cadet uniforms for using in JROTC.

name: cdrip
description: Local Bun-based codebase that runs a webserver and then allows you to rip any CDs in your system using cdparanoia.

name: cdmaker
description: Tool that creates fully readable CD images (bin/cue) given a directory of WAV files. I made it because I couldn't find any other tooling that worked out of the box without needing any dependencies or weird setup beforehand, while this script only requires you to have Python 3.

name: pdb
description: This is the site you are actively reading off of, pretty basic frontend for a massive ass JSON(c) file that has all my project data on it.

name: metrics
description: FastAPI based ASGI system that records Cloudflare site metrics, including TTFB, TLS Handshake Time, TLS Connection Time, and more. Can be ran from a customizable amount of nodes all around the world, with a 1-4 limit.

name: carbon
description: Asynchronous NoSQL database that uses SQLite as a backend for data storage. It has full support for replication and was a fun experiment to work on.

name: radon
description: An attempt at a decentralized network for sending encrypted data between peers.

name: ghost
description: Work-in-progress self-hosted privacy-respecting analytics server designed to provide nothing but a visit count.

name: cmd
description: Web terminal complete with games, social interaction via email, a typing test, baud rate emulation, and more.

name: rei
description: CLI meant to assist with everything from managing projects to updating DNS records.

name: genrerip
description: Tool to automatically find accurate music genres via MusicBrainz and LastFM.

name: shadow
description: HTTP/1.1 compliant ASGI system, meant to replace HTX (2508).

name: snorlax
description: A YouTube archival service, all running locally. It supports downloading videos as well as entire channels, displaying it in a fairly decent interface.

name: iavx
description: The iiPython AV1 Encoding System. As of 03/01/26 it's been used to encode around 10 different shows.

name: sniffer
description: Automatically trace all externally linked domains on a website and display them in a node tree.

name: pixel-os
description: New work-in-progress web based operating system, with full modding support.

name: cyanide
description: Central report system for K-12 schools in Mississippi, ranking vulnerabilities by risk.
